title: The "rewrite this worse" trick
subtitle: A 3-minute move for instantly cleaner AI writing.

blocks.markdown:
AI outputs have a predictable house style: over-polished, vaguely corporate, and longer than needed. You can feel it when you read it — _"delving into,"_ _"comprehensive solutions,"_ _"in today's rapidly evolving landscape."_

There's a 30-second move that fixes this more reliably than any other prompt trick: tell the model to _write it worse on purpose, then keep the good parts._

blocks.prompt: Rewrite your previous response in a bluntly human voice. Imagine a smart friend wrote it in 90 seconds. Short sentences. No marketing words. Cut anything that sounds like it was written to impress.
blocks.annotation: The rough tone instruction lets the model break out of its default register. Most of the time, the "bluntly human" version is 90% of what you wanted — you just take that and smooth it slightly.

blocks.title: Why it works
blocks.body: Models have a strong default bias toward "professional" register. Asking for plainness directly doesn't fully counteract it. Asking for something _slightly rougher than you actually want_ does, because you're pulling hard on the other end.

blocks.instruction: Take an AI-written paragraph from your recent chats (or generate one now — ask ChatGPT or Claude for a short product update). Run the rewrite instruction above. Paste the cleaner version below.

blocks.question: You apply this trick and the rewrite goes _too_ rough — it now sounds sloppy, not plain. What's the next move?

choices.text: Give up and accept the original AI draft.
Take the rewrite's structure but manually edit 2-3 lines that went too casual.
Run the trick again, more aggressively.
Ask the model to "be more professional."

blocks.explanation: The trick's power isn't in the AI's output — it's in the _axis_ it gives you to edit along. The rewrite pulls the draft toward human; your final pass pulls it back toward polished. You can't get there by asking "be more professional" without sliding back into the default house style.
